title: Daily Market Digest: April 12, 2026
subtitle: Market closes week with gains amid geopolitical developments and inflation data

items.headline: Markets Closed for Weekend; Friday Saw Mixed Close, Strong Weekly Gains
items.summary: U.S. equity markets are closed today, Saturday, April 12, 2026. The previous trading day, Friday, April 11, concluded with a mixed performance across major indices as investors weighed ongoing U.S.-Iran ceasefire talks and recent inflation data. Despite a cautious Friday, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite recorded their best weekly gains since November. Overall market sentiment remains influenced by geopolitical tensions, energy prices, and the anticipation of Q1 earnings season, which kicks off next week. Not financial advice.

items.statContext: Down 0.1% on Friday, Up 3.6% for the week
Down 0.6% on Friday, Up 3% for the week
Up 0.4% on Friday, Up 4.7% for the week

items.headline: Nvidia Extends Winning Streak
items.summary: Nvidia ($NVDA) climbed 2.6% on Friday, continuing an eight-day winning streak, its longest since November 2023. Other chipmakers also saw gains.

items.headline: Organon Surges on M&A Speculation
items.summary: Organon ($OGN) soared 27.8% on Friday following reports of a potential $12 billion acquisition offer.

items.headline: Verastem Experiences Decline
items.summary: Verastem ($VSTM), a biopharma company, saw its stock price decline by 5.01% on Friday.

items.headline: BridgeBio Oncology Therapeutics Down
items.summary: BridgeBio Oncology Therapeutics ($BBOT) decreased by 5.14% on Friday.

items.headline: Inflation Data Highlights Energy Prices and Fed Outlook
items.summary: Consumer prices rose 3.3% annually in March, with core CPI at 2.6%. The PCE price index, the Fed's preferred measure, increased by 2.8% in February, with core PCE at 3%. High energy prices due to the Middle East conflict are cited as a significant driver of inflation. The market is not currently pricing in any Fed rate cuts for 2026, despite earlier guidance for one cut. Consumer sentiment has also dropped to multi-decade lows.

items.headline: Middle East Ceasefire Talks Influence Oil Prices and Supply Chains
items.summary: U.S.-Iran ceasefire talks have led to a slip in oil prices, though the broader conflict in the Middle East continues to impact global supply chains and raise concerns about a permanent scar on the global economy, according to the IMF.

items.headline: Q1 Earnings Season to Begin Next Week with Major Banks
items.summary: The first-quarter earnings season is set to commence next week, with major financial institutions including Goldman Sachs ($GS), JPMorgan ($JPM), Citigroup ($C), Bank of America ($BAC), Morgan Stanley ($MS), and Wells Fargo ($WFC) scheduled to report. Netflix ($NFLX) is also on the docket. Optimism regarding earnings guidance, particularly from the information technology sector and semiconductors, has been noted.

items.headline: Industrials and Consumer Cyclicals Led Weekly Sector Gains
items.summary: For the week ending April 10, the Industrials sector was the best performer, rising 5.60%, followed by Consumer Cyclical, up 5.50%. Energy was the worst-performing sector, down 3.77%, while Healthcare saw a modest gain of 0.25%. In Q1 2026, Energy was a clear standout, gaining 37.91%.
